calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-24-2008 Dismissal Or Judgment On File As To All Defendants

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-31-2008 Continued To Oct-17-08 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 212

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Aug-14-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Steven Weinstein, Esq. appeared for plaintiff U-Save Equipment Rental, Inc., a California corporation.
James Lew, Co-Owner of U-Save Equipment Rental, Inc., was placed under oath and examined.
No appearance by defendants Shamrock Structures, Inc., a California corporation or Banya 2000, LLC, a California limited liability company.
Defaults entered against defendants on June 30, 2008.
Doe defendants dismissed on July 23, 2008.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff U-Save Equipment Rental, Inc., a California corporation, and against the Defaulted Defendants Shamrock Structures, Inc., a California corporation and Banya 2000, LLC, a California limited liability company as follows:

Damages/Principal - $21,761.08.
Prejudgment Interest - $1,254.30.
Costs - $568.02, for a total monetary judgment in the amount of $27,782.88.

Furthermore, plaintiff U-Save Equipment Rental, Inc. has lien upon the real property owned by defendants, located at 748 Innes Avenue, San Francisco, CA. The property is subject to a Claim of Lien dated March 25, 2008. A true and correct copy of the lien is attached as Exhibit A to the Judgment. The property shall be sold at public auction by the Sheriff of the City and County of San Francisco, according to law. The Sheriff shall retain out of the proceeds of the sale his or her fees, disbursements, and commissions on the sale, and pay to plaintiff U-Save Equipment Rental, Inc. out of the proceeds the amount of this judgment.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
